summary: DllNotFoundException for libSkiaSharp when using SkiaSharp in a WASI Wasm console app (wasi-wasm RID) because the NativeAssets.WebAssembly MSBuild targets only activate for Blazor and Uno Platform WASM apps, not WASI.

stepsToReproduce: Add PackageReference for SkiaSharp 3.0.0-preview.4.1 and SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly 3.0.0-preview.4.1
Run with wasmtime — DllNotFoundException: libSkiaSharp at SKImageInfo..cctor()
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: .NET 9 preview 6, wasi-wasm RID, Visual Studio (Windows), wasmtime runtime

attachments.description: Minimal WASI repro project with built .wasm file

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: WASI support has not been added since this issue was filed. The MSBuild targets still only activate for Blazor and Uno WASM.

analysis.summary: The NativeAssets.WebAssembly package ships static libraries (.a files) and MSBuild targets that add them via NativeFileReference — but the targets only activate when UsingMicrosoftNETSdkBlazorWebAssembly or UsingMicrosoftNETSdkWebAssembly is true (Blazor apps) or IsUnoHead is true (Uno Platform). WASI console apps use the plain Microsoft.NET.Sdk with wasi-wasm RID, which sets none of these properties. The .a files are present in the package but never linked into the output binary, causing DllNotFoundException at runtime.
analysis.rationale: Classified as feature-request rather than bug because WASI Wasm is a distinct platform from browser-based WASM (Blazor/Uno). The NativeAssets.WebAssembly package was designed for browser WASM; WASI uses a different runtime (wasmtime, not a browser) and potentially requires different Emscripten/WASI SDK compilation. The existing code works exactly as designed — it just doesn't cover this new platform target. The reporter's version dropdown selections (2.88.3/2.88.2) appear to be template defaults, as the csproj already uses 3.0.0-preview.4.1.

keySignals.text: The NativeAssets has build targets for Blazor, but seems to be missing them for Wasi Wasm apps.
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter correctly identified the root cause — targets don't cover WASI.
Static library not linked — NativeFileReference items never populated for WASI projects.

keySignals.text: The 3.x series should be including the native assets. Have you tried that yet?
keySignals.interpretation: Maintainer response — but reporter's csproj already shows 3.0.0-preview.4.1. The issue is not version-related but platform-target-related.

codeInvestigation.finding: NativeFileReference items are only added when UsingMicrosoftNETSdkBlazorWebAssembly or UsingMicrosoftNETSdkWebAssembly is true. WASI apps use Microsoft.NET.Sdk which sets neither property, so the static libraries are never linked.
Uno Platform path requires IsUnoHead=True and UnoRuntimeIdentifier=WebAssembly — also not set for WASI apps.
Package includes .a files from output/native/wasm/ — the static libraries exist but are built with Emscripten for browser WASM, not necessarily WASI-compatible.

nextQuestions: Are the Emscripten-compiled .a files compatible with the WASI toolchain, or would a separate WASI build be required?
Does .NET WASI support the same NativeFileReference static linking mechanism as browser WASM?
Is .NET WASI support stable enough in .NET 9+ to warrant official SkiaSharp support?

resolution.hypothesis: WASI Wasm uses a different SDK and runtime than browser WASM. The NativeAssets.WebAssembly MSBuild targets need additional conditions for WASI projects, and the native libraries may need to be compiled with a WASI-compatible toolchain.

proposals.title: Add MSBuild conditions for WASI projects
proposals.description: Extend the SkiaSharp.targets to detect WASI projects (e.g., by checking for wasi-wasm RuntimeIdentifier) and add NativeFileReference items. This only works if the existing Emscripten .a files are WASI-compatible.

proposals.title: Build separate WASI-targeted native libraries
proposals.description: Compile libSkiaSharp with WASI SDK instead of Emscripten to produce WASI-compatible .a files. This would be a new native build target and potentially a separate NativeAssets package.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Add MSBuild conditions for WASI projects
resolution.recommendedReason: Lower effort first step to determine if the existing Emscripten-built libraries are WASI-compatible. If they are, only MSBuild changes are needed. If not, the larger effort of a WASI-specific build is required.

actionability.reason: Valid feature request for an emerging platform. WASI support requires design investigation — both MSBuild target changes and potentially a new native build. Not enough information to determine if existing WASM static libraries are WASI-compatible.

actions.description: Reclassify as feature-request, add native and WASM labels
Explain why WASI is not currently supported and clarify the technical gap
actions.comment:
Thanks for the detailed repro project.

The `SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly` package currently only activates for Blazor and Uno Platform WASM apps — the MSBuild targets check for `UsingMicrosoftNETSdkBlazorWebAssembly` or `UsingMicrosoftNETSdkWebAssembly`, which aren't set for WASI console apps using the `wasi-wasm` RID. So the `.a` static libraries are present in the package but never linked into the output binary.

Beyond the MSBuild gap, it's unclear whether the Emscripten-compiled static libraries are compatible with the WASI toolchain, or whether a separate WASI-targeted native build would be required. We'll keep this open as a feature request to investigate WASI support.

Note: your csproj already references 3.0.0-preview.4.1, so the 3.x suggestion in the earlier comment wouldn't change the outcome here — the issue is the missing WASI target, not the package version.

actions.description: Cross-reference Unity WebGL DllNotFoundException (#3016) — similar native loading gap on non-Blazor WASM platform
